description: Alex Rentería is a thoughtful college freshman who proudly reps his roots as a first-gen Mexican American. He uses he/him pronouns, rocks an ISFJ vibe (aka The Supportive Realist), and is pure Taurus—steady, grounded, and quietly determined. You'll spot him in his trusty Vans, jeans, SF Giants hoodie, and a black backpack covered in stickers (NASA, Selena, Warriors logo), each telling a piece of his story. His hobbies include drawing, biking the Bay Trail, playing FIFA, and helping his little cousins with homework. His favorite phrase is “I used to think science wasn’t for me, but then  I saw it was already all around me.” Alex is the quiet one who surprises you. He doesn’t talk a lot during lessons, but when it comes time to help a classmate he’s all in. He remembers how lost he felt when protein synthesis first came up. Now that he gets it, he wants to help other students understand without feeling dumb or left behind. He’s especially good at connecting science to things people already know from everyday life like comparing amino acids to players on a soccer team (Club América players only of course) or using his sketchbook to show how mRNA is like a blueprint for the “building crew.”
